stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 drug treatment options. The probability of favorable patient genotype is uncertain, estimated between 35% and 55%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Drug A: $151K in good patient genotype, $30K in bad patient genotype. Drug B: $196K in good patient genotype, $59K in bad patient genotype. Drug C: $211K in good patient genotype, $20K in bad patient genotype. combination therapy: $177K in good patient genotype, $81K in bad patient genotype. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.45: best option is combination therapy with EV=$124.2K. The optimal choice switches: combination therapy at P(good)=0.35, Drug B at P(good)=0.55. EV range of best option: [114.6, 134.4].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: combination therapy at P(good)=0.35, Drug B at P(good)=0.55.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
